Get Your Solar Proposal

The customer journey

Eight Clear Steps From Proposal to Power

Every solar project has moving parts. Our process keeps the energy analysis, property, documentation and approvals connected.

Home/Our Solar Process

How the journey works

Know what happens, who handles it and what you receive.

The exact timing varies, but the structure below makes responsibilities and outputs easier to understand.

01

Step 1

Perfect Proposal

Customized around your electricity usage, property and energy goals.

What you provide

Your recent electricity bills, property address and energy goals.

What we handle

We review usage patterns and organize the information needed to discuss system fit.

What you receive

A proposal direction with clearly identified inputs and open questions.

02

Step 2

Savings Report

Clear estimated production, bill-offset and long-term savings analysis.

What you provide

Any known future loads, operating schedules or major property changes.

What we handle

We explain estimated production, bill offset and the assumptions behind the analysis.

What you receive

A readable savings report that separates estimates from guarantees.

03

Step 3

Approval Process

We guide the required financing, utility and project approvals.

What you provide

Requested identification or finance information only through the approved secure process.

What we handle

We guide the applicable project, utility and financing approval workflow.

What you receive

A clear list of decisions, conditions and next actions.

04

Step 4

Documentation

We organize the forms and project information required to move forward.

What you provide

Accurate property and account information for the required forms.

What we handle

We organize project documentation and keep required information connected.

What you receive

A documented project file ready for the next technical step.

05

Step 5

Site Survey

Roof, electrical, shading and installation conditions are professionally reviewed.

What you provide

Safe site access and relevant roof, building or electrical history.

What we handle

We review roof, electrical, shading, access and installation conditions.

What you receive

Confirmed site observations that inform the final design.

06

Step 6

CAD / Permit

Technical drawings and required permit documentation are prepared.

What you provide

Timely review of design questions when choices affect the property.

What we handle

We prepare technical drawings and coordinate required permit documentation.

What you receive

An approved design package, subject to authority requirements.

07

Step 7

Installation

Your solar equipment is installed according to the approved project design.

What you provide

Site access and coordination around the agreed installation schedule.

What we handle

We install the system according to the approved design and site plan.

What you receive

A completed installation ready for required inspection and commissioning.

08

Step 8

System Activation

After final approval, your system is commissioned and monitoring is configured.

What you provide

Access for final checks and the monitoring setup process.

What we handle

We support final approval, commissioning and monitoring configuration.

What you receive

An activated system and a clear view of how to monitor it.

Property conditions, financing, utility review, permit processing, equipment availability and inspection schedules can affect the sequence and timing.

Installation with context

The approved design stays connected to the real site.

Survey findings, roof conditions, electrical equipment and access planning all inform how the installation moves from paper to property.

See Installation Work

Understand the proposal

A savings report built on your energy use—not a generic promise.

We organize the assumptions that shape a solar proposal so you can see how consumption, estimated production and bill offset relate.

  • Twelve-month usage profile
  • Property and system assumptions
  • Estimated production pattern
  • Bill-offset explanation
  • Variables that can change
  • Clear next-step questions

Actual results depend on energy usage, system design, weather, roof or site conditions, utility rates, export credits, equipment, approvals and customer behaviour.

Get Your Solar Proposal

Proposal insight

Energy profile overview

Electricity useSolar production
Usage reviewed
Site-fit inputs
Assumptions explained

Illustrative report structure — not customer results

Process questions

What can affect the project timeline?

Approvals and permits

Utility, permit and inspection timelines are controlled by the applicable organizations and can vary by property and season.

Property conditions

Roof work, electrical upgrades, structural questions, access constraints or design revisions can affect sequencing.

Customer decisions

Equipment choices, financing, documentation and timely approvals can influence how quickly a project advances.

Weather and scheduling

Safe installation conditions and coordinated site access remain important throughout construction and activation.

Your next step

Turn Sunlight Into a Smarter Energy Plan

Share your electricity usage and property details. We’ll organize the information needed to shape a clear solar proposal.

Get Your Solar Proposal
Based in Calgary, Alberta

Solar solutions for Alberta homes, businesses and farms, from the first conversation to system activation.
